column3: Issue
column4: Statement A
column5: Statement / instrument / record B
column6: Conflict
column7: Significance

column3: Trust pleaded under the 2024 amendment, not its founding deed
column4: O.S. 243/2024 Plaint, Dated 14.08.2024, Para 1 (filed by D2 as Managing Trustee, suing the plaintiff): pleads the trust is "constituted as per the registered deed No. 17/IV/2024" — presenting a 2024 amendment as the founding instrument.
column5: Trust Deed No. 2/IV/2007, Dated 04.01.2007 is the actual constituting deed; D2 himself admits this in Reply (IA 5/2025), Dated 16.02.2026, Para 3, that the trust was "registered on 04-01-2007" at SRO Cheppad.
column6: Trust pleaded as founded on the 2024 amendment, not the 2007 deed.
column7: Pleaded deed (17/IV/2024) is not the constituting deed (2/IV/2007).

column3: Trust "constituted 2024" yet owns property settled in 2022
column4: O.S. 243/2024 Plaint, Dated 14.08.2024, Para 1: pleads the trust was "constituted as per … No. 17/IV/2024", placing its creation in 2024.
column5: Same Plaint, Para 2: claims the trust owns the suit property under Settlement Deed No. 1669/I/2022, Dated 10.11.2022 — property settled to the trust in 2022, two years before the 2024 creation it pleads.
column6: A trust said to be constituted in 2024 is pleaded as owning property settled to it in 2022.
column7: Impossible unless the trust predates 2024; confirms 17/IV/2024 is not the constituting deed.

column3: Active trust, mandatory accounts duty, zero account documents
column4: O.S. 243/2024 Plaint, Dated 14.08.2024, Para 4 (D2's own suit): the trust had "employees deployed by the plaintiff trust" at the site on 09.08.2024 — activity falling in FY 2024-25.
column5: Reply (IA 5/2025), Dated 16.02.2026, Paras 4-6 & 13 (D2 on oath): the trust was a "dormant entity" with "no … revenue … whatsoever" (FY 2007-2024) and could produce nothing but a single passbook.
column6: Trust deployed employees in FY 2024-25, yet swore dormancy and produced no account document for any year.
column7: If "2007-2024" covers FY 2024-25, the oath is false; if not, an active trust kept no accounts (breach; adverse inference).

column3: Documents sworn "not available," then produced
column4: Reply (IA 5/2025), Dated 16.02.2026, Para 13 (D2 on oath): the documents the plaintiff sought in IA 5/2025 were "not available for production".
column5: D2 Affidavit (IA 18.05.2026), Dated 18.05.2026, Para 3: the same D2 then produces the account No. 13100200004482 statement, the Original Minutes Book and the trustee resolutions — the very records earlier sworn unavailable.
column6: Documents sworn unavailable in February were produced three months later.
column7: Contradicted by the deponent's own production.

column3: Lease deed withheld, then produced
column4: Reply (IA 5/2025), Dated 16.02.2026, Paras 8 & 13 (D2 on oath): the trust's only post-2024 record is a passbook; everything else is "not available for production".
column5: D2 Affidavit (IA 18.05.2026), Dated 18.05.2026, Para 3: D2 produces the lease he himself executed on 27.10.2025 with Sree Moolavasam Wellness Pvt. Ltd. — an instrument in his own hand, omitted from the earlier account.
column6: A lease in the deponent's own hand was omitted, then produced.
column7: A known instrument omitted from his account, produced later.

column3: Production timed against the HC disposal order
column4: HC Order, OP(C) 3278/2025, Dated 15.01.2026: directed the trial court to dispose IA 2, 5 & 9 of 2025 within three months (i.e. by about 15.04.2026).
column5: D2's Reply (IA 5/2025), Dated 16.02.2026 swore the records "not available", on which footing IA 5/2025 was closed (Order, Dated 04.03.2026); D2 produced them only on 18.05.2026, after the recess and the deadline.
column6: IA 5 closed on "not available"; the same records produced after the HC window.
column7: Closure procured by an untrue "not available" affidavit; compliance nominal; production timed to defeat the order.

column3: O.S. 243/2024 cause of action — plaintiff had left India
column4: O.S. 243/2024 Plaint, Dated 14.08.2024, Para 6 (D2's suit): the cause of action arose 13.08.2024 on "reliable information" that the plaintiff was making "hasty preparations to trespass … again" — an apprehended future act.
column5: Passport (Doc 10, O.S. 214/2025): the plaintiff's exit stamp is 10.08.2024 and he departed India on 11.08.2024 (having entered 02.08.2024) — already abroad before the date of the apprehended future trespass.
column6: Plaint apprehends a future trespass on 13.08.2024; plaintiff had already left India on 11.08.2024.
column7: A future act is pleaded against a person who had left the country; immigration record makes it impossible.

column3: "Ongoing construction" pleaded, but the building was finished and inaugurated
column4: O.S. 243/2024 Plaint, Dated 14.08.2024, Paras 3, 5 & 6 (D2's suit): pleads "renovation works … are going on" and seeks to protect the "ongoing construction" — the very act the injunction is meant to guard.
column5: The renovated Meda was completed by November 2023 and publicly inaugurated on 23.12.2023 (the trust's own ceremony, arranged by the MT [Exhibits I1/I2]), open since — no construction was ongoing by August 2024.
column6: The injunction's pleaded basis — "ongoing construction" — had ended months before the suit.
column7: A fabricated construction premise; the trust inaugurated the finished building eight months before pleading it unfinished.

column3: No accounts for 18 years despite a mandatory duty
column4: Trust Deed No. 2/IV/2007, Dated 04.01.2007, Clause 2: the Managing Trustee is "bound to maintain all records and accounts of the trust" — a mandatory duty.
column5: Reply (IA 5/2025), Dated 16.02.2026, Paras 4-5 (D2 on oath): "no books of account were maintained" for 2007-2024 — an admitted 18-year failure of that duty.
column6: Managing Trustee admits 18 years with no accounts, against the deed's duty.
column7: Inconsistent with the deed's accounts duty; cf. ITA 1882 ss.19, 57, 23.

column3: Trust amended though the deed bars amendment
column4: Trust Deed No. 2/IV/2007, Dated 04.01.2007 (certified): contains no power for the authors or trustees to amend, vary the beneficiaries, or revoke the trust.
column5: D2's conduct: he nonetheless executed Amendment Deeds No. 66/IV/2022 and No. 17/IV/2024, altering the beneficiary class and enlarging trustee powers — without any power to amend and without all beneficiaries' consent.
column6: Trust twice amended with no power to amend and no beneficiary consent.
column7: No amendment/revocation power; cf. ITA 1882 ss.11 & 78. Challenged as void.

column3: Beneficiary status made to turn on a "nomination" invented by amendment
column4: D2 Affidavit (IA 18.05.2026), Dated 18.05.2026, Para 3: D2 produces a "Nomination as beneficiary" document, treating beneficiary status as something to be conferred by nomination.
column5: Trust Deed No. 2/IV/2007, Dated 04.01.2007 makes beneficiaries the descendants of Chellamma Channathy by descent and has no nomination mechanism; the "nomination" requirement appears only in the later Amendment Deed No. 17/IV/2024.
column6: The amendment makes beneficiary status turn on the father's nomination — a requirement the founding deed never imposed.
column7: The fixed beneficiary class cannot be narrowed by amendment (ss.11, 78 ITA); the nomination requirement is void.

column3: Plaintiff sworn to be "not a beneficiary"
column4: Reply (IA 5/2025), Dated 16.02.2026, Para 11 (D2 on oath): "The plaintiff is not a beneficiary" of the trust, so he cannot maintain the suit.
column5: Trust Deed No. 2/IV/2007, Dated 04.01.2007, Page 3: beneficiaries are the "descendents of … Chellamma Channathy"; D2 himself admits the plaintiff is "S/o Radhakrishnan" (O.S. 243/2024 Plaint, Dated 14.08.2024, Para 4), and Radhakrishnan is Chellamma's son.
column6: Deed defines the class; Radhakrishnan is Chellamma's son, the plaintiff his son.
column7: Inconsistent with the founding deed and the admitted parentage.

column3: Beneficiary branded a stranger and trespasser
column4: O.S. 243/2024 Plaint, Dated 14.08.2024, Para 4 (D2's suit): the plaintiff has "no … connection with the plaintiff trust" and "is not a member", and is sued as a trespasser.
column5: Trust Deed No. 2/IV/2007, Dated 04.01.2007, Page 3: beneficiaries are the "descendents of … Chellamma Channathy"; the plaintiff is admittedly "S/o Radhakrishnan", her son — squarely within the beneficiary class.
column6: A beneficiary under the deed is pleaded as a stranger.
column7: Inconsistent with the beneficiary class in the founding deed.

column3: Invited to the trust inauguration, then branded an outsider months later
column4: Invitation by D4 (the Managing Trustee's wife), 23.12.2023 [Exhibits I1/I2]: a trustee-side invitation brought the plaintiff to the Meda inauguration, where he lit the inaugural lamp — treated as family.
column5: O.S. 243/2024 Plaint, Dated 14.08.2024, Para 4 (just eight months later): the same plaintiff is pleaded to have "no … connection with the plaintiff trust" and is branded an outsider and trespasser.
column6: Welcomed to a trust event as family in Dec 2023; pleaded a stranger with "no connection" by Aug 2024.
column7: Conduct treating the plaintiff as family is inconsistent with the later "no connection" pleading. [Exhibit: invitation.]

column3: Undisclosed health-care business on the premises
column4: Reply (IA 5/2025), Dated 16.02.2026, Paras 8 & 13 (D2 on oath): for the post-2024 period the only disclosure is a passbook; everything else is "not available".
column5: GST Registration, GSTIN 32ABQCS9416G1Z1 (effective 09.12.2025): Sree Moolavasam Wellness Pvt. Ltd. (incorporated 07.08.2025, after the suit) is shown "Active", running residential health-care services from the trust premises — undisclosed activity on the property.
column6: A GST-registered business runs on the premises in the period disclosed as "only a passbook."
column7: Sworn statement inconsistent with a Government (GST) record.

column3: Public Meda inauguration held while the trust was sworn dormant
column4: Reply (IA 5/2025), Dated 16.02.2026, Paras 4 & 6 (D2 on oath): the trust had "no … revenue … whatsoever" and was a "dormant entity" throughout FY 2007-2024.
column5: Meda Inauguration, 23.12.2023 [Exhibits I1/I2]: the trust's renovated Meda was publicly inaugurated (trustee-side invitations, inaugural lamp lit) — an organised event with evident activity, falling inside the very dormancy window sworn above.
column6: A "dormant", no-revenue trust held a public inauguration of its renovated building in Dec 2023.
column7: Activity inside the sworn dormancy window; inconsistent with "dormant"/"no revenue". [Exhibits: invitation, ceremony.]

column3: Dormant/no-accounts trust, yet the trustee acquired adjoining land
column4: Reply (IA 5/2025), Dated 16.02.2026, Paras 4-6 & 13 (D2 on oath): the trust was "dormant", with "no … revenue … whatsoever" and its records "not available for production".
column5: Registered Sale Deeds [Exhibits M1-M5]: during the same period the Managing Trustee (and D4) acquired land adjoining the trust [R.S. 219/20, 219/25, 219/26, 219/5; D4 219/4 — verify], raising an unexplained conflict of interest while the trust was kept opaque.
column6: A trustee who swears the trust kept no accounts is unaccountable for acquisitions adjoining the trust.
column7: Raises a conflict of interest (ITA ss.51, 14); MT to disclose adjoining acquisitions and their funding via discovery.

column3: D5: "Meda Construction Supervisor" vs. "no connection"
column4: Crime 574/2024 (now C.C. 1055/2024): the complainant D5, Arun B. Unni, styles himself "Alummoottil Meda Construction Supervisor" to prosecute the plaintiff — asserting an official trust-site role.
column5: D5 Objection, Dated 18.02.2026, Para 3 (same D5, on oath): he has "no … connection with … Trust" and was merely a "worker of the contractor … for the 3rd respondent".
column6: D5 claims a Meda post to prosecute a beneficiary, but denies any trust connection on accounts.
column7: Self-contradictory sworn statements by D5. (Arrest stayed: Crl.M.C. 5800/2025.)

column3: Trespasser headcount differs between plaint and FIR
column4: O.S. 243/2024 Plaint, Dated 14.08.2024, Para 4 (D2's suit): the plaintiff allegedly trespassed "along with 5 other men" — six intruders in all.
column5: FIR 574/2024 (lodged by D5, Arun B. Unni, on the same 09.08.2024 incident): records the plaintiff and only 2 others — three in all. The defendants' two accounts differ by three persons.
column6: The plaint and the FIR give different counts for the same 09.08.2024 incident.
column7: Inconsistent accounts of the incident underlying the criminal case; goes to credibility.

column3: Who started the fight on 09.08.2024?
column4: O.S. 243/2024 Plaint, Dated 14.08.2024, Para 4 (D2's suit): the plaintiff "brutally manhandled" three "employees deployed by the plaintiff trust" — casting the plaintiff as the aggressor.
column5: FIR 574/2024 / D5's statement (Arun B. Unni) [verify wording]: D5 reacted first on seeing the plaintiff, and admits he knew the plaintiff as Radhakrishnan's son and opened the gate on the plaintiff's mother's call — neither a stranger nor, on his own account, the one attacked first.
column6: Plaint makes the plaintiff the aggressor; D5's own account has him admitting, then confronting, the plaintiff.
column7: The plaintiff's side, not D5's, called the police. Who made first contact, and who was in danger?

column3: Nature of the injuries
column4: O.S. 243/2024 Plaint, Dated 14.08.2024, Para 4 (D2's suit): the plaintiff "brutally manhandled" three trust employees — alleging the plaintiff inflicted the violence.
column5: Medical records [verify]: the plaintiff suffered a critically injured shoulder requiring surgery, while D5-D7 show no injuries consistent with an attack (D5 only a wrist scratch) — the serious injury is the plaintiff's.
column6: The only serious injury is the plaintiff's; the alleged victims show none.
column7: The injury pattern is inconsistent with the plaintiff as aggressor. Who attacked whom?

column3: "Trust employees" are D3's contractor's workers
column4: O.S. 243/2024 Plaint, Dated 14.08.2024, Para 4 (D2's suit): the persons at the site were "employees deployed by the plaintiff trust".
column5: D5-D7 Objection, Dated 18.02.2026, Para 3 (those very persons, on oath): they were "the contractor's … for the 3rd respondent" and had "no connection" with the trust — i.e. D3's men, not the trust's.
column6: Persons pleaded as trust employees swear they worked for D3 personally.
column7: Mutually exclusive sworn statements; works placed under D3.

column3: D6 & D7: "no connection," yet present in trust property
column4: D6 & D7 Objection, Dated 18.02.2026, Para 3 (on oath): they "have no connection with … Trust", denying any role with it.
column5: Crime 956/2024 (the plaintiff's complaint vs D2, D3, D5, D6, D7 + 1) and photographs: D6 and D7 are named as present at the trust premises during the 09.08.2024 incident.
column6: D6/D7 deny any trust connection, yet are named present at the Trust premises on 09.08.2024.
column7: Mutually exclusive accounts; who admitted D6 and D7 to the gated premises? [Confirm 956/2024.]

column3: Construction — the trust's, or D3's personal project?
column4: O.S. 243/2024 Plaint, Dated 14.08.2024, Para 4 (D2's suit): the construction was being carried out "at the instance of plaintiff trust" — a trust project.
column5: Reply (IA 5/2025), Dated 16.02.2026, Paras 6 & 7(b) (D2 on oath): the trust was "dormant" and D3 was to fund the works "from his personal accounts" per the Trustee Resolution, Dated 12.02.2020 — i.e. D3's personal project.
column6: Works are the trust's in one suit, D3's personal activity in the other.
column7: Mutually exclusive on oath. cf. ITA 1882 s.66 (blending).

column3: Lease registered outside the jurisdictional registry
column4: D2 Affidavit (IA 18.05.2026), Dated 18.05.2026, Para 3: the Sree Moolavasam lease was "registered through the Marad, Alappuzha Sub-Registrar's Office" — outside the registry covering the trust property.
column5: The trust property and Trust Deed No. 2/IV/2007 fall under Cheppad SRO (Maradu is in Ernakulam, not Alappuzha); HC Judgment, WP(C) 31247/2025, Dated 27.08.2025 had just directed Cheppad SRO to furnish the plaintiff certified copies of the trust deeds.
column6: Lease registered away from Cheppad SRO — the registry the HC had just directed to serve the plaintiff.
column7: Registered elsewhere to evade scrutiny; defect rests on R2's own admission (ss.28/49 Registration Act); jurisdiction seriously questionable.

column3: Bank account opening date
column4: Reply (IA 5/2025), Dated 16.02.2026, Para 8 (D2 on oath): the trust's first account (Federal Bank, a/c 13100200004482) was "opened on 26/05/2025".
column5: D2 Affidavit (IA 18.05.2026), Dated 18.05.2026, Para 3 (same deponent): the same account was "opened on 28/05/2025" — two different sworn opening dates for the one account.
column6: Two sworn opening dates for the trust's first account.
column7: Reconcile against the bank record.

column3: Date error in the evidence affidavit
column4: D2 Affidavit (IA 18.05.2026), Dated 18.05.2026, Para 3: describes the bank statement as running "from 28/05/2005 …", placing it twenty years before the account existed.
column5: D2 Affidavit (IA 18.05.2026), Dated 18.05.2026, Para 3 (same paragraph): states the account was opened 28/05/2025 — exposing "2005" as an internal date error.
column6: Statement dated to 2005, before the account existed.
column7: Obtain a corrected copy.

column3: Managing Trustee's age
column4: Reply (IA 5/2025), Dated 16.02.2026: the Managing Trustee's age is given as "77".
column5: Order, Dated 04.03.2026 records "78"; and D2 Affidavit (IA 18.05.2026), Dated 18.05.2026 records "79" — the same person ages two years across roughly three months of filings.
column6: Age rises 77 → 78 → 79 in ~3 months.
column7: Particulars only.

column3: Managing Trustee's parentage
column4: Reply (IA 5/2025), Dated 16.02.2026: the Managing Trustee gives his parentage as "S/o Madhavan Thurail Krishnan Channar".
column5: O.S. 243/2024 Plaint, Dated 14.08.2024 (cause-title, same person): "S/o late T.K. Madhavan" — a different father's name in the two sworn filings.
column6: Father's name differs in the dormancy affidavit.
column7: Particulars only.r""."
column8: O.S. 243/2024 Plaint, Dated 14.08.2024 (cause-title, same person): "S/o late T.K. Madhavan" — a different father's name in the two sworn filings.
column9: Father's name differs in the dormancy affidavit.
